title: Amauropelta consanguinea
intro: Amauropelta consanguinea, also known as the mountain fern, is a species of fern native to the Andes mountain range. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing pain and inflammation, as well as protecting against oxidative stress and cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Amauropelta consanguinea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpene lactones, which have been identified as responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Amauropelta consanguinea is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues.
